channel.title: Lambda on Matt Weagle
channel.description: Recent content in Lambda on Matt Weagle

item.title: Serverless, Serverfull, and Weaving Pipelines
item.description: Serverless computing is a hot topic these days. Depending on the tweet-weather, it’s a cloud computing revolution, a ripe source of lock-in, a net cost savings or an unpredictable cost driver, a stepping stone to event-sourced applications, the commoditization of containers, and a host of other things. Or maybe it’s just a full circle return to the glory days of Perl and cgi-bin. Putting aside the tweet-bait, there’s a general agreement that running a “serverless service” definitely doesn’t signal the End of Ops.

item.title: See Lambda Go
item.description: The Sparta framework for AWS Lambda provides a full-featured environment for writing go-based, Serverless-powered applications. Up until version 0.11.1 however, there was a measurable overhead to this approach, especially in container cold-start scenarios. Cold-starts happen when a serverless function of any type needs to be created, placed, and invoked by a provider. For Sparta applications, this meant a relatively large performance penalty before the NodeJS-based shim was able to forward the request to the standalone go binary.
